page: OVHcloud t2-le-180 is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for compute-heavy and parallel processing workloads. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6226R processor, delivering 60 dedicated physical cores running at 2.9 GHz with one thread per core. The system is equipped with 180.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

The defining feature of this instance is its graphics acceleration hardware, which consists of four NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs providing a combined 128 GB of HBM2 VRAM. This hardware configuration delivers top-tier performance in large language model (LLM) inference benchmarks. Specifically, prompt processing speeds reach 15,337.57 tokens per second for a small 135M model, 1,308.79 tokens per second for a medium 7B model, and 152.1 tokens per second for a large 70B model. Text generation speeds also rank in the top tier for medium and large models, achieving 119.58 tokens per second and 18.53 tokens per second respectively.

On the CPU and memory front, the dedicated 60-core architecture yields strong multi-core performance. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 324.83 GB/sec, and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is rated as strong at 60,701.4. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1,480.7 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1,239.0, reflecting an average tier. Memory bandwidth is also balanced, with a strong PassMark memory score of 2,794.65 but average read and write speeds for large uncached blocks.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the t2-le-180 offers high resource density by bundling four enterprise-grade GPUs with a high-core-count dedicated CPU on a KVM hypervisor. This makes it highly efficient for workloads that can fully exploit parallel GPU processing, such as deep learning training, LLM inference, and complex scientific simulations. Conversely, for workloads relying purely on single-threaded CPU performance or requiring massive system memory per core, the instance presents a tradeoff due to its moderate single-core speeds and 3.0 GB RAM-to-core ratio.
description: OVHcloud t2-le-180 is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6226R processor. It delivers 60 dedicated physical cores running at 2.9 GHz, paired with 180.0 GB of system memory and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The hardware configuration is highlighted by four NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs, providing a total of 128 GB of VRAM. This setup excels in parallel processing, demonstrating top-tier performance in LLM inference benchmarks, including prompt processing speeds of 152.1 tokens per second for 70B models. While multi-core CPU and memory benchmarks are strong, single-core performance remains average. This instance is highly cost-efficient for GPU-heavy workloads, making it best suited for deep learning, LLM inference, and parallel scientific computations, though its 3.0 GB RAM-to-core ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive single-threaded tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud t2-le-180 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 60 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 180 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA V100S GPUs with 128 GB VRAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference and parallel computing.
meta_description: OVHcloud t2-le-180 is a GPU-accelerated server with 60 dedicated Intel cores, 180 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA V100S GPUs optimized for LLM inference.
tagline: A high-density GPU server featuring four NVIDIA V100S accelerators and sixty dedicated cores for demanding parallel computing and machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs providing 128 GB of total VRAM.
Sixty dedicated Intel Xeon 6226R physical cores running at 2.9 GHz.
Top-tier LLM inference performance across small, medium, and large models.
180.0 GB of system memory and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization.
Optimized for deep learning, AI inference, and highly parallel scientific simulations.
